name: The Boundary Beyond
intro_text: Mexico City is fracturing. The past is bleeding through into the present — ancient Tenochtitlán overlapping with the modern city, ruins appearing where buildings should be, canals running through streets.
The Eztli relic is causing a temporal collapse. You must navigate both the 1921 city and its ancient Aztec counterpart simultaneously to find the source and seal the rift.
Every clue you gather in one era may reveal something hidden in the other. But the doom clock advances regardless — the barrier between past and present grows thinner with every passing minute.
setup_instructions: Each location has a present-day and ancient version. Place the present-day side face-up initially.
When an investigator discovers the last clue on a location, flip it to its ancient side — the location now represents Tenochtitlán.
Serpents of Yig and Brotherhood cultists appear in both eras.
The scenario ends when investigators seal the temporal rift (advance Act 3) or time collapses completely (doom reaches the final threshold).

resolutions.R1: You seal the temporal rift. Mexico City stabilises. Record in the campaign log: *The investigators sealed the boundary.* Each investigator earns 2 experience.
resolutions.R2: The rift seals partially but the damage is done. Record: *The boundary was partially sealed.* Each investigator earns 1 experience.
resolutions.defeat: Time collapses and the Eztli ruins swallow the city. Each investigator suffers 1 physical trauma and 1 mental trauma.
